findings.reachability: Any authenticated user with access to any wp-admin page can trigger this via a GET request or CSRF img-tag embed; no capability check is performed beyond being in an admin context.
findings.justification: The admin_notices handler checks isset($_GET['shfs_dismiss_notice']) and immediately calls delete_option('shfs_script_access_level') without verifying a nonce or checking current_user_can(); a forged GET request (e.g., via an img src pointing to wp-admin/index.php?shfs_dismiss_notice=true) silently suppresses the migration security warning that prompts admins to review permission settings.

detection_pattern.section_name: CSRF: GET-triggered option deletion without nonce
detection_pattern.description: Catches code that acts on a query-string flag to delete or modify options without a nonce, allowing CSRF deletion of WordPress options from any admin page load.

findings.location: HeaderAndFooterScripts::update_role_author() lines 248-255, update_role_contributor() lines 263-270, triggered by update_option hooks from the settings form
findings.reachability: Site administrators who enable the 'Allow Authors add scripts' or 'Allow Contributors add scripts' checkboxes in plugin settings; the capability grant persists in the role table permanently.
findings.justification: The plugin's UI states it allows Authors/Contributors to 'add scripts' via the meta box, but the implementation calls $role->add_cap('unfiltered_html') on the entire role globally: this grants the WordPress 'unfiltered_html' capability that bypasses wp_kses_post() in the block/classic editor for ALL posts authored by that role, enabling injection of arbitrary <script> tags in post content far beyond just the plugin's meta box, which constitutes a scope-exceeding privilege escalation.

detection_pattern.section_name: Privilege escalation: unfiltered_html granted to non-admin roles
detection_pattern.description: Flags code that grants the unfiltered_html capability to Author or Contributor roles, which allows arbitrary HTML/JS injection in post content beyond the plugin's intended scope, and is especially dangerous on multisite where unfiltered_html is intentionally withheld from all but super-admins.

findings.location: HeaderAndFooterScripts::wp_head() line 174, 179; wp_body_open() line 190; wp_footer() line 205
findings.reachability: All site visitors; the stored scripts are echoed verbatim in page output. The write-path is gated by current_user_can('unfiltered_html') at line 320, but on WordPress multisite this capability is restricted to super-admins only; on single-site, ordinary admins have it by default and the plugin optionally grants it to Author/Contributor roles.
findings.justification: All three output hooks echo option values and post meta without any escaping (phpcs:ignore comments confirm this is intentional); while gating input on unfiltered_html is the correct approach for a script-injection plugin, the optional privilege escalation to Author/Contributor roles means lower-privilege users can inject arbitrary JavaScript served to all visitors, and the wp_footer path additionally runs stored content through do_shortcode() which can execute registered shortcode callbacks stored within the scripts.

detection_pattern.section_name: Stored XSS: unescaped echo of option/meta values
detection_pattern.description: Catches plugins that suppress PHPCS escape warnings and echo option or post-meta values verbatim to page output; combined with any write-path that grants unfiltered_html to non-admin roles this becomes an exploitable stored XSS.

findings.reachability: The footer script content is stored by admins (or lower roles if privilege escalation is enabled) and runs through do_shortcode() on every page load for all visitors.
findings.justification: The footer script value is passed to do_shortcode() before output (lines 201-205), while the header and body scripts are not; this inconsistency means any registered shortcode (including those added by other plugins) embedded in footer scripts executes as a callback, creating an indirect code-execution surface if a malicious shortcode is registered or if the stored content was injected by an attacker who exploited the Author/Contributor privilege escalation finding.

detection_pattern.section_name: Shortcode execution on admin-stored option values
detection_pattern.description: Flags plugins that run get_option() values through do_shortcode() on front-end output, which can execute arbitrary registered shortcode callbacks and amplifies the impact of any stored-content injection finding.
